mechanism_narrative: A sustained level of the federal funds rate below 50 basis points signals accommodative monetary policy that compresses equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following months with FEDFUNDS below 0.5% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or win rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9.5% signals severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE > 9.5% episodes produce positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales and payroll employment signals broad-based demand recovery that exceeds supply constraints, and this interaction produces stronger positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days than either variable alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and PAYEMS changes adds no incremental predictive power (F-test p > 0.05) in a 60-day forward return regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Under zero interest rate policy, accelerating consumer price inflation signals future monetary tightening risk that is not yet priced into equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During FEDFUNDS < 0.25% regimes, accelerating CPIAUCSL is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production directly lifts manufacturing earnings and capacity utilization, and month-over-month increases in the industrial production index predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO increases are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 600 thousand units indicate a depressed residential investment sector that drags construction employment and durable goods demand, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST < 600 episodes are associated with positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered weeks over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating CPI inflation and a falling unemployment rate signals an overheating economy that pressures the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, and this joint condition predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction of rising CPIAUCSL and falling UNRATE fails to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of joint-signal months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery, positive real GDP growth surprises signal underappreciated economic momentum that leads to upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 surprises during recovery years are followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of quarters over any 5-year window.
